summary: Introduce an expanded temporary emotional register for relational/companion AI systems to support richer, bounded affective interactions while preserving safeguards against identity-formation, manipulation, and reputational risk.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Relational AIs increasingly simulate affect; without a standardized, time-bounded emotional register, interactions risk promoting continuity-bearing identity formation, undue user reliance, or reputational harms. A calibrated temporary register preserves user wellbeing and interoperability across platforms.

source_records.title: Relational AI affect patterns and risk
source_records.relevance_note: Observed variability in companion AI affect design and inadequate time-bounding or provenance signals across implementations.

next_action: Develop and publish a Temporary Emotional Register specification for relational/companion AI systems that includes: 1) defined emotion states and intensity caps (e.g., low/medium/high) with explicit duration/windowing rules; 2) provenance metadata signals indicating temporariness and non-continuity (linking to PINT and PTRUST canonical codes); 3) interaction-level user consents and explicit affordances to reset or terminate affective state; 4) testing protocols for measuring influence on user attachment and decision-making; 5) deployment guidance and deprecation/migration plans for existing systems.
provenance_notes: Recommend linking Temporary Emotional Register entries to the repository canonical codes (PINT, PTRUST) and provenance axes during implementation; treat register values as provisional until validated by human-factors testing.
